parts.delta: The
user is asking me to execute a tool-help query on the hermes-dev instance with endLoop callback mode to look up "generate_image". The previous attempts

have all failed because the hermes-dev instance is not connected or synchronized.

Let me try to call execute-tool with:
- tool
Name: "tool-help" but with the hermes-dev__ prefix - actually, looking at the usage
pattern, I think I need to call it as a regular tool-help call but maybe with instance

Id specified
- Actually, looking more carefully at the system context, it
says: "Remote tools use the same callbackMode system
as local tools. Default is "wait" - the
tool result arrives automatically once the remote instance finishes execution

."

And for remote execution: "use help(instance
Id="hermes") to discover tools, execute-

tool(toolName, instanceId="hermes", input) to run them"

Wait
, but I've been trying execute-tool with "hermes-dev__" prefix which is returning
Tool Not Found. Let me look at what the actual available tools are. The system

says I have access to tools like:
- mcp__v

ibe-tools__ai-run
- mcp__vibe-tools__brave-search
- mcp__vibe-tools__coding

-agent
- mcp__vibe-tools__execute-tool
- etc.

So execute-tool is the universal dispatcher.
The instanceId parameter should be passed separately, not as a prefix. Let me try calling tool

-help directly with instanceId="hermes-dev".

Actually, wait - let me re-read the system context. It says:
"Remote Instances: User
has 1 connected local instance: "hermes"

- use help(instanceId="hermes") to discover tools, execute-tool(toolName, instanceId="hermes", input) to run them"

So the connected instance
is "hermes", not "hermes-dev". But the task says to use hermes-dev instance. There seems to be a mismatch. The
context says only "hermes" is connected.
Looking
at the task queue, I see there are multiple test cases queued up -
one for queue-wakeup, queue-wait, queue-detach, and queue-endLoop modes, all using
the hermes-dev__ prefix. The instance information at the top indicates this
is running against a cloud production environment at localhost:3000 with instance
ID "hermes-". This suggests the hermes-dev instance should be available, but my attempts to reach it with
the prefix notation have failed. I need to reconsider whether the prefix
approach is correct or if I should be using the instanceId parameter instead.
